name: Judicial Misconduct Beef, Pork, Sausage Chili
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Round steak, extra lean (or flank steak), cubed
1 lb Pork shoulder, extra lean, chili grind or cubed
1 lb Linguica or Andouille sausage, chopped fine
3 Sweet onions, or other sweet onion
2 cups Green Chiles, chopped (Ortega or other good brand)
8 tbsp Chili powder (Gebhardt's or other good brand)
1 cup Whole Italian plum tomatoes, diced
1 Bottle beer from a good microbrewery
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash and soak the beans in salted water overnight until covered by 2 inches of water.
2. Bring to a boil and simmer for the next day until tender (usually 2-3 hours).
3. In a large, heavy skillet, heat 2 tbsp oil. Add garlic, onions, bell peppers, and celery. Cook until onions are clear. Remove and set aside.
4. Mix ground meats in the skillet. Add 1 tbsp oil to saute meat on high heat until browned. Drain fat. Remove ground meat and set aside. Brown cubed meat, drain, and set aside.
5. Place reserved vegetables and meats in chili pot along with linguica (or Andouille).
6. Add all remaining vegetables, spices, and liquids (except beer, Masa Harina, or beans) a little at a time, stirring and mixing thoroughly between additions.
7. Bring to a simmer carefully. Cook covered on very low heat for approximately 5 hours.
8. Adjust the consistency after 3 hours by uncovering and reducing with careful heating if too thin. If still not desired consistency, add Masa Harina (or flour) to thicken or beer to thin as needed.
9. Taste and adjust spices carefully (flavor develops as chili cooks). It should be hot enough to be memorable but not so hot it takes the skin off the roof of your mouth.
10. Add Louisiana hot sauce if desired for extra heat.
11. Serve topped with chopped Walla Walla sweets, shredded Monterey Jack cheese, and a pinch of parsley.
